bonuses.leadin: For his Anthropometries series, Yves Klein painted nude models this color and had them make imprints on a blank canvas as “human paintbrushes.” For 10 points each:
parts: Name this color that Klein developed a namesake international shade of using ultramarine. Pigments of this primary color can be produced from lapis lazuli.

Klein created a headless plaster cast of this sculpture completely covered in blue. Alexander of Antioch is believed to have created this armless sculpture that features 28 times in Dalí’s The Hallucinogenic Toreador.
These two artists were both part of the Nouveau réalisme movement founded by Yves Klein. This husband-and-wife duo designed large landmark projects such as the Wrapped Reichstag.
answers: Christo and Jeanne-Claude [or Christo Vladimirov Javacheff and Jeanne-Claude Denat de Guillebon; prompt on partial answer]
answers_sanitized: Venus de Milo [or Aphrodite of Melos]
Christo and Jeanne-Claude [or Christo Vladimirov Javacheff and Jeanne-Claude Denat de Guillebon; prompt on partial answer]
